competitors.name: Rolz
features: Real-time chat room for rolling with others
No login required for basic use
weaknesses: Outdated, early-2000s visual design
No visual dice rolling animation
Limited customization of dice appearance
Interface can feel cluttered with ads and chat
No persistent history or save/export features

competitors.name: Wizards of the Coast Dice Roller
features: Pre-configured rolls for D&D 5e (Ability Checks, Attacks, etc.)
Simple and intuitive for new players
weaknesses: Very limited to D&D 5e, not generic
Cannot roll multiple dice types simultaneously in one 'throw'
No advanced notation or logic
No collaborative features
Requires a (free) Wizards account to save characters

competitors.name: Random.org Dice Roller
features: Strong reputation for fairness and legitimacy
Extremely simple: one button for standard dice
Multiple dice displayed as a list of results
weaknesses: Functionally very basic
Only rolls standard polyhedral dice one type at a time
No customization, notation, or advanced features
No visual rolling simulation, just static results
UI is sparse and not engaging for gaming

competitors.name: Dice Roll by Google
features: Supports standard dice (d4-d20) and a coin
Zero friction, no website to visit
weaknesses: Extremely limited feature set
No multi-dice or custom notation
No history, logging, or sharing
Cannot be embedded or used in a dedicated tab
Completely dependent on Google's product whims

competitors.name: RPGBOT Dice Roller
features: Supports complex dice expressions and logic
Helpful examples and documentation on site
weaknesses: No real-time collaborative features
Dice are graphical but animation is basic (fade-in)
Interface has many options which can overwhelm casual users
Branding is tied to the RPGBOT blog/network
No user accounts or saved presets

gaps.gap: Lack of integrated, persistent virtual tabletop (VTT) rooms for sessions.
gaps.implementation: Build a tool where a GM can create a room, share a link, and all players' dice rolls appear in a shared log with player labels. Include a simple text chat and ability to save room state.

gaps.gap: No tool combines advanced dice logic with a highly satisfying and customizable visual/audio experience.
gaps.implementation: Create a physics-based 3D dice roller with customizable dice textures, colors, and materials. Add impactful sound effects (clatter, bounce) and haptic feedback options for mobile. Allow users to build and save 'dice sets'.

gaps.gap: Missing 'smart' dice rollers with built-in game systems (beyond D&D 5e).
gaps.implementation: Include pre-configured rollers for popular systems like Pathfinder 2e, Call of Cthulhu, Warhammer, and board games. Feature would auto-calculate modifiers, difficulties, and interpret special dice (e.g., Star Wars/Genesys narrative dice).

gaps.gap: Poor mobile experience for quick, repeated rolls during a physical game.
gaps.implementation: Develop a progressive web app (PWA) with a 'quick roll' dashboard. Users can pin 4-6 favorite dice expressions to their home screen for one-tap rolling. Optimize for ultra-fast loading and offline use.

gaps.gap: No analytics or insights for game masters on player/game luck.
gaps.implementation: For logged-in users in a session, provide a simple dashboard showing roll distribution (e.g., 'The party rolled 35% below average tonight'), streak tracking, and exportable roll logs for recap blogs/podcasts.

recommended_positioning: The collaborative virtual dice tray for online tabletop RPGs, combining satisfying physics-based rolls with seamless session management.
